mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate that remains near a cyclical trough and has not resumed a hiking trend continues to support equity valuations through a low discount-rate channel, producing modestly positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as the market prices continued accommodation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.24-0.36 range in Dec 2015, just off zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-rate periods show no return premium versus rising-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0% while continuing to decline, the labor market is interpreted as reaching full employment, raising market expectations of accelerated Fed tightening and compressing forward equity returns over the next quarter through higher discount rates.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0% and falling for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns following sub-5% UNRATE triggers are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in real retail sales signals resilient consumer demand, which propagates into near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage_value 448086 Dec 2015 vs revised 442149, showing continued nominal growth
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive skew conditional on positive RSAFS momentum across a 3-year rolling window (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained year-over-year decline in industrial production signals an emerging manufacturing recession that historically precedes broader earnings deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter once the decline exceeds a meaningful threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised_value 99.0417 vs vintage 106.0222 in Dec 2015, indicating manufacturing softness
failure_condition: Instances of INDPRO YoY decline below -1.0% are followed by positive 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth combined with a rising unemployment rate signals a turning point in the labor cycle that precedes equity market drawdowns, because the two series together capture both quantity and rate-of-change labor slack that neither series alone reliably detects.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change decelerates by >20% while UNRATE rises >=0.2pp over same window
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised_value 143077 vs vintage 143242 Dec 2015 showing slight downward revision
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS deceleration and UNRATE rise trigger conditions produce 20d SPX forward returns that are not significantly negative (p>0.10) or occur with fewer than 15 triggered instances across full walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a simultaneous decline in housing starts and a rising federal funds rate reinforce each other because higher rates directly suppress mortgage-sensitive housing demand, and the combined weakening signals broader consumer retrenchment that depresses equity returns over the next quarter more than either signal alone.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines >5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS is rising
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS hiked to 0.24-0.36 range Dec 2015 marking start of tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns during joint HOUST decline and FEDFUNDS rise triggers are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over either series individually.

mechanism_narrative: Stable, low headline CPI inflation near the Fed's implicit target reduces uncertainty about future policy tightening, supporting risk asset valuations and producing a persistent modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised_value 238.017 close to vintage 238.111 in Nov 2015, indicating low revision risk and stable low inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC between CPIAUCSL YoY stability and 20d forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or high-inflation-volatility periods show equal or higher returns than low-volatility periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp downward revision or deceleration in real GDP growth below trend signals weakening aggregate demand and raises recession probability, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next trading week as markets rapidly reprice growth expectations upon release.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage_value 16414.0 vs revised_value 18857.418 in Jul 2015, showing large high revision risk
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following sub-1.5% GDPC1 growth prints are not significantly negative (p>0.10), or GDPC1 high revision risk causes signal reversal in more than 40% of instances upon later vintage comparison.
